CH 01 — SIGNAL OPEN
The internet has social graphs.
It doesn't have a broadcast layer.
Yet.
Built by Reverie Collective
CH 02 — THE GAP
The internet gave everyone a voice.
It never gave anyone a channel.
You can post. You can go live for a moment. You can upload a video and hope the algorithm finds it. But there is no way to maintain a persistent, always-on broadcast that actually belongs to you. Social media is episodic. Broadcasting has always been continuous.
CH 03 — WHAT IT FEELS LIKE
Public access TV.
For the internet.
You don't log on. You tune in. There's always something on. Drop in at any time — the channel is running whether you're there or not. Ambient presence. Shared time. A signal that doesn't disappear when the creator steps away.
CH 04 — WHAT MEGALITH IS
Run a 24/7 broadcast channel
from your own domain.
Infrastructure, not platform. Creator-controlled. Always on.
01
Upload
Add video to your channel vault
02
Schedule
Loop, queue, or time-block your programming
03
Broadcast
Channel runs continuously — always on air
04
Tune in
Drop in anytime. Something's always happening.

Built for signal holders.
A
Artists
24/7 channel as practice, archive, and living presence

Brands
Owned broadcast layer. No algorithm tax, no rent.
C
Collectives
Community systems with shared, programmable broadcast
D
Institutions
Galleries, labels, publishers — always-on ambient presence

Always something on.
Live now on MGLTH.TV

CH 08 — BUSINESS MODEL
Not ad-based.
Not algorithm-based.

Channel deploy
